title: AI MYTHBUSTERS
subtitle: Real or Myth? Test your AI knowledge.

questions.statement: AI works using artificial neural networks inspired by the way our brain connects information.
questions.funFact: Neural networks are inspired by biological neurons. They use layers of connected nodes to identify patterns, similar to how our brain processes information — but they do not think or feel like humans.

questions.statement: AI learns by itself like a human child learning from experience.
questions.funFact: AI learns through mathematical algorithms and large amounts of data provided during training. It does not have curiosity, emotions, or understanding like humans.

questions.statement: The more high-quality data an AI model learns from, the better it can become.
questions.funFact: AI models improve by finding patterns in large datasets. Better quality data usually helps AI produce better results.

questions.statement: ChatGPT does not "search the internet"; it predicts the most likely next words based on patterns it learned.
questions.funFact: Large Language Models (LLMs) generate responses by predicting sequences of words based on patterns learned during training.

questions.statement: AI has a brain like humans and can form its own opinions.
questions.funFact: AI does not have consciousness, beliefs, emotions, or personal opinions. It generates responses based on patterns in data.

questions.statement: AI can predict the future perfectly.
questions.funFact: AI predicts possibilities using patterns and probabilities. It can make useful forecasts, but unexpected events can still happen.

questions.statement: Voice assistants understand words the same way humans understand conversations.
questions.funFact: AI recognizes patterns in speech and language. It does not truly understand meaning, emotions, or context the same way humans do.

questions.statement: AI can accidentally become biased and make unfair decisions.
questions.funFact: AI learns from data. If the training data contains bias, the AI system may learn and reproduce those biases.

questions.statement: AI remembers every conversation it has ever had with every user.
questions.funFact: AI systems do not automatically remember every interaction forever. Memory depends on how the AI system is designed and configured.

questions.statement: AI is a new invention that started only after ChatGPT launched.
questions.funFact: AI research has existed since the 1950s. ChatGPT is one of the recent breakthroughs that made AI popular among everyday users.

questions.statement: Bananas are commonly used to train AI models because they are easy for AI to recognize.
questions.funFact: Bananas are sometimes used in AI demonstrations because they are simple objects to recognize, but they are not special AI training material.

questions.statement: Hugging Face is a popular platform where people share AI models and tools.
questions.funFact: Hugging Face is a major open-source AI community where researchers and developers share machine learning models, datasets, and AI tools.

questions.statement: Gemini's logo means magic.
questions.funFact: Google Gemini's logo uses a sparkling, abstract design to represent intelligence, creativity, and the idea of AI helping humans discover and create new things. The name "Gemini" refers to two connected concepts working together.

questions.statement: Nano Banana is a new type of AI-powered banana used to train AI models.
questions.funFact: Nano Banana is actually a nickname used online for an AI image generation/editing model, not a real fruit or AI training material. It became popular because of its unusual name.

questions.statement: Hugging Face is a social media app where AI researchers share selfies.
questions.funFact: Hugging Face is an AI platform and open-source community where developers share AI models, datasets, and machine learning tools.

questions.statement: Microsoft Copilot is called "Copilot" because AI is designed to assist humans, not replace them.
questions.funFact: A copilot helps a pilot fly an aircraft by assisting with navigation and decisions. Similarly, AI copilots are designed to support humans in tasks like writing, coding, and analysis.

questions.statement: Large Language Models (LLMs) understand language exactly like humans do.
questions.funFact: LLMs are extremely good at recognizing language patterns and generating responses, but they do not truly understand concepts, emotions, or experiences like humans.

questions.statement: Generative AI can create completely new content even though it learned from existing examples.
questions.funFact: Generative AI learns patterns from existing data and uses those patterns to create new text, images, music, videos, and code.

questions.statement: AI models have billions of "parameters" that help them make decisions.
questions.funFact: Parameters are values learned during training that help AI recognize patterns and generate outputs. Large models can contain billions or even trillions of parameters.

questions.statement: Prompt engineering means teaching an AI model how to think like a human.
questions.funFact: Prompt engineering means creating better instructions or questions to guide AI tools. It does not change the AI's actual thinking process.

questions.statement: AI uses more electricity than the entire internet.
questions.funFact: AI systems do require significant computing power, especially during training, but the relationship between AI energy use and global internet energy use is more complex.

questions.statement: The first AI chatbot was created before the first mobile phone became popular.
questions.funFact: ELIZA, one of the earliest chatbots, was created in 1966 — decades before modern smartphones and today's AI assistants.

questions.statement: AI was first tested by teaching computers to recognize cats.
questions.funFact: A famous Google research experiment in 2012 used neural networks to identify cats from millions of images.

questions.statement: AI-generated artwork has won competitions against human artists.
questions.funFact: AI-generated art has competed and won recognition in creative competitions.

questions.statement: The word "robot" existed before AI was invented.
questions.funFact: The word robot came from a 1920 Czech play, while modern AI research began later.

questions.statement: AI can help scientists discover new medicines faster.
questions.funFact: AI can analyze molecules and predict potential drug candidates.
